title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 54% of over 350 readers support U.S. military strikes on Iran, while 43% oppose and 3% are unsure.
Supporters cite Iran's role in regional conflicts and describe the Iranian government as a 'brutal regime.'
Opponents fear escalation into broader war, criticize lack of congressional approval, and prefer focus on domestic issues.

title: Paul Pierce tells Celtics fans to keep expectations in check over Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advises Celtics fans to manage expectations for Jayson Tatum's return from Achilles injury.
Tatum's return expected to include a minutes restriction to manage his recovery.
Tatum may contribute overall but not be the main scorer during the Celtics' playoff push.

title: Patriots are reportedly releasing WR Stefon Diggs after 1 season in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots release WR Stefon Diggs, freeing $16.8 million in cap space for 2026.
Diggs exceeded 1,000 receiving yards with Patriots in 2025 and was crucial in reaching Super Bowl LX.
Legal issues and $26.8 million cap hit contributed to Diggs' release after one season in New England.

title: High court judge denies DiZoglio’s ask for outside attorney in audit lawsuit
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Judge Dalila Argaez Wendlandt denies State Auditor Diana DiZoglio's request for outside counsel in a lawsuit concerning legislative audit compliance.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell moves to strike the lawsuit, arguing DiZoglio lacks authority to sue without her office's approval.
DiZoglio must file a response by Thursday, as she pursues a separate ballot initiative for legislative transparency under public records law.

title: Former school bus driver indicted in Hyde Park crash that killed 5-year-old boy
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jean Charles indicted for involuntary manslaughter, reckless and negligent homicide in death of Lens Joseph.
Crash occurred April 28, 2025, when Charles allegedly missed the correct bus stop, leading to the accident.
Boston school bus system operator Transdev criticized for poor record-keeping and incomplete driver training.

title: Wedding photo company’s sudden bankruptcy leaves more than 100 Mass. couples scrambling
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yours Truly Media filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy, leaving over 100 Massachusetts couples without wedding photos or deposits returned.
Owners Paul and Amy Bolton reported over $2 million in business-related debts and listed 478 couples among creditors in bankruptcy documents.
Massachusetts Attorney General's Office received 10 complaints against the company between May 2020 and March 2026.

title: Plan a beach getaway around a concert at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom in New Hampshire is planning a multi-million dollar redevelopment starting after the 2027 summer season.
The project will expand the venue to a 3,500-seat event space, with a boutique hotel, condominiums, casino, and parking garage.
Artists performing in 2026 include Melissa Etheridge, Clint Black, Lita Ford, The 502s, David Lee Roth, and The Fab Four.

title: Bucket truck overturns into oncoming traffic; worker seriously injured
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A bucket truck overturned into oncoming traffic on Memorial Drive, Cambridge, seriously injuring a worker.
The incident occurred around 9 a.m., leading to a closure from North Harvard Street to Memorial Drive.
The State Police and OSHA are investigating; the road is expected to stay closed for several hours.

title: Healey congratulates WHOOP on plans to expand, add 600 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: WHOOP plans to add 600 jobs, mainly at its Kenmore Square headquarters, expanding its presence in Boston.
Governor Maura Healey praised WHOOP's expansion as a vote of confidence in Massachusetts' economy and talent.
WHOOP will offer new roles in technical, scientific, creative, and commercial areas across global locations, including North America and Asia.

title: Here’s the Eagles’ reported asking price for a Patriots A.J. Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Eagles want a 'Quinnen Williams-type deal' for A.J. Brown, suggesting a first-round pick with a second-round pick sweetener.
A.J. Brown recorded 78 catches for 1,003 yards and seven touchdowns in the 2025 season with the Eagles.
Brown's strong relationship with Patriots coach Mike Vrabel could influence trade discussions between the Patriots and Eagles.
